_comment: Ceilings for bench/run.py --check, enforced in CI.
requests: the exact number of API calls a route makes on a cold open. A change here is a view
starting or stopping a call, which should always be deliberate.
gzip_bytes: a ceiling, not a measurement, set about 30% above what the route costs today.
Headroom is deliberate: zlib versions differ slightly in output size between machines, and a
benchmark that fails on that teaches people to ignore it. Regressions worth catching are far
Update these in the same commit as any change that moves them, with the reason in the message.
feed_requests: how many pages the list pulls after its first. The list asks for the rows it is
about to draw plus a page of slack, so this is 1 at every size -- it used to be 25 at ten
thousand issues, pulling 179 KB nobody had asked to see. A jump means either that demand
calculation changed or paging stopped making progress (it once looped 2,824 times against a
Note how little of this grows with the tracker now: only #/graph, which genuinely draws every
issue. The other three cost about the same at ten thousand issues as at a hundred, because
none of them reads anything whose size is the size of the tracker any more.
